title: Weekly 1 on 1 with Michael D and Joe F
summary: CRM visualization and database complexity were discussed. Foundational system fixes are prioritized via a coordinated strategy with Vinnie. Major CRM deficiencies need addressing for marketing and lead generation. Michael Donovan to present CRM foundational fixes to Vinnie.

decisions.description: Foundational CRM work is required before proceeding with system development.
Major CRM deficiencies must be addressed to improve marketing strategy and lead generation.
Michael Donovan will prioritize and present necessary CRM foundational fixes to Vinnie.
All email campaigns and social posts will be delivered in a 'nice neat tidy spot'.

actionItems.task: Upload existing contact lists; classify as MQLs.
Speak with Vinnie about better approaches for CRM coordination.
Present proposed CRM foundational fixes to Vinnie tomorrow.

expectedOutcome.description: Approval for foundational CRM work to support lead generation.

actionItems.task: Include a reasonable timeline for required completion of CRM fixes.
Balance and finalize the proposed recap format against the initial carousel idea.
Confirm Vinnie's attendance for the meeting tomorrow.
Address major deficiencies in the CRM to improve marketing and lead generation.

expectedOutcome.description: Improved marketing strategy and lead generation.

actionItems.task: Ensure email campaigns and social posts adhere to the established review process.

expectedOutcome.description: Timely review and approval of marketing content.

tensions: Extreme difficulty navigating the complex CRM visualization and finding specific relationships.
A disconnected line in the CRM visualization highlighted the messy nature of the system.
Michael Donovan refuted the idea that CRM issues were 'better reporting', insisting on '101 fundamentals'.
Joe Furnari raised concerns about deviations from agreed-upon processes for email campaigns.
Lack of adequate review time for Vinnie on last week's email campaign violated established process.
Difficulty wrestling with Zoho, taking over four and a half hours for email tasks.
referencesToPast: Joe Furnari reminded the team they were asked to create a road map last week.
Joe Furnari clarified the issue with last week's email campaign regarding review time.
Michael Donovan jokingly implied Paulo Trovao's use of Firefox was 'stuck in the past'.

contributions.description: Presented the expansive, complex CRM relational database map.
Pointed out a disconnected line in the CRM visualization, highlighting messiness.
Stated the system is not ready without defining necessary fixes and ownership.
Agreed major CRM deficiencies must be addressed for marketing and lead generation.
Suggested approaching Vinnie to coordinate collaborative CRM improvement efforts.
Refuted issues as 'better reporting', insisted on a lack of '101 fundamentals'.
Raised critical issue of website performance wasting Google ad money.
Reminded team to create a roadmap prioritizing items for best outcomes.
Advised Michael Donovan to present CRM foundational work as a priority to Vinnie.
Brought up deviations from agreed-upon processes for email campaigns.
Confirmed email campaigns and social posts will be delivered in a 'nice neat tidy spot'.
Noted personalization requires Zoho Cadence, unusable without system fixes.
Clarified issue with last week's email was lack of adequate review time.
